pr.title: Enable inline_auto, fix FObj match, and mark GObj_GXReorder as dont_inline
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Enabled CodeWarrior automatic inlining globally by adding `-inline auto` to `CFLAGS`, then repaired matching fallout in baselib by replacing a fake unrolled `HSD_FObjRemoveAll` implementation with the direct recursive version and preventing `GObj_GXReorder` from being inlined into `GObj_SetupGXLink` via local `#pragma dont_inline` guards.
what_changed: Added `-inline auto` to the global `CFLAGS` line in `Makefile`, alongside existing CodeWarrior options such as `-O4,p`, `-fp_contract on`, and `-proc gekko`.

Removed the prior fake-match structure in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.c`, including inline helper `HSD_FObjGetNext`, inline helper `HSD_FObjRemoveAll_Inlined`, and a hand-unrolled `HSD_FObjRemoveAll` that freed up to several linked-list elements manually.
Replaced `HSD_FObjRemoveAll` with a compact recursive implementation: return on `NULL`, recurse on `fobj->next`, then call `HSD_FObjRemove(fobj)`.
Wrapped `GObj_GXReorder` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c` with `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`.
The PR body states this fixed a fake FObj match caused by a weird unrolled loop and marked `GObj_GXReorder` as `dont_inline` because otherwise it was inlined into `GObj_SetupGXLink`.
smart_moves: Used the compiler's `-inline auto` behavior instead of preserving a fake source-level unrolled loop in `HSD_FObjRemoveAll`, making the C source more natural while maintaining the intended match.
Applied `#pragma dont_inline` narrowly around `GObj_GXReorder` rather than disabling automatic inlining globally or for the entire file.
Used `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` to localize the inline-control state, reducing risk of accidentally changing inlining decisions for later functions in `gobjgxlink.c`.
Converted the FObj cleanup routine from a brittle fake match to a readable recursive linked-list teardown that still delegates removal to `HSD_FObjRemove`.
decomp_lessons: When enabling a global compiler behavior such as `-inline auto`, expect some previously matched functions to change shape and require targeted fixes.
Fake matches that mimic compiler artifacts, such as manual unrolling, may become unnecessary or harmful once the correct compiler inline setting is used.
For functions that must remain standalone under automatic inlining, prefer local CodeWarrior pragmas over broad build-flag workarounds.
Recursive linked-list cleanup may be the intended source pattern even when previous matching attempts looked like explicit multi-node unrolling.
Always guard local pragma changes with push/pop when the pragma is only intended for a single function.
naming_conventions: Baselib FObj routines use the `HSD_FObj*` prefix, as seen in `HSD_FObjRemove`, `HSD_FObjFree`, `HSD_FObjRemoveAll`, and `HSD_FObjSetState`.
Baselib GObj GX-link routines use `GObj_` names for local implementation functions such as `GObj_GXReorder` and `GObj_SetupGXLink`.

Temporary fake-match helper names such as `HSD_FObjRemoveAll_Inlined` and `HSD_FObjGetNext` were removed, suggesting helpers introduced solely for matching should be deleted when compiler settings make them unnecessary.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Enable CodeWarrior automatic inlining with `-inline auto` when matching code that depends on compiler-selected inline behavior.
Use `#pragma dont_inline on` for a single function when global automatic inlining would otherwise inline it into its caller.
Use `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` around `#pragma dont_inline on` to keep inline settings scoped.
Replace manual unrolled fake source with natural recursive source when the compiler flag configuration can generate the desired code shape.
The specific matching issue recorded here was that `GObj_GXReorder` would inline into `GObj_SetupGXLink` unless explicitly marked `dont_inline`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that adjust `-inline auto` fallout or add `#pragma dont_inline` for functions newly inlined by CodeWarrior.
Search for remaining fake-match helper functions with `_Inlined` suffixes that may be removable after compiler flag corrections.
Audit other baselib linked-list cleanup routines for natural recursive implementations versus manual unrolled fake matches.
Search for `GObj_GXReorder` and `GObj_SetupGXLink` history to confirm no later changes altered this inline-control tactic.
